title: Image-Layered World Model
description: Third-person video decomposition for reasoning and control
summary: Third-person video generation can be more useful if it separates persistent scene state, object/contact dynamics, and robot/ego dynamics instead of treating video as flat pixels.
asset_caption: Persistent scene, object/contact, and robot/ego layers.
details: WEM is the closest current conceptual neighbor: it separates instruction-agnostic world evolution from instruction-conditioned robot ego dynamics for long-horizon navigation-manipulation rollout.
The project delta should be a finer manipulation decomposition: scene/background layer, object/contact transition layer, and robot/ego action layer rather than only world versus ego.
Target outputs: data augmentation, reward model, and inverse dynamics model.
Robot layer should support robot priors at generation time: either a manipulator URDF or a single robot-arm image as the robot-condition input.
Immediate work: survey layered video / object-centric generation methods and borrow WEM-style long-horizon phase metrics where useful.
risks_decisions: Layered model scope should move beyond visual decomposition toward state-aware scene / object-contact / robot-ego dynamics.
Object/contact state is the bridge between WEM's world and ego; if ignored, generated videos can look stable while failing manipulation physics.
Evaluation target: data augmentation quality, reward-model usefulness, IDM recoverability, and long-horizon phase consistency.
